title: The Magic of Compound Interest - The 8th Wonder of the World
description: Let's learn about the power of compound interest, which Einstein called 'The 8th Wonder of the World'!

scenes.speech: Compound interest is like magic! Let's experience it together ✨
scenes.title: The Magic of Compound Interest

scenes.speech: What's the difference between simple interest and compound interest?
scenes.title: Simple Interest vs Compound Interest
scenes.content: Simple Interest: Interest only on the principal. Compound Interest: Interest on both the principal and the interest! Example: $1,000,000 at 10% interest for 10 years. Simple Interest → $2,000,000. Compound Interest → approximately $2,590,000! A difference of $590,000.
scenes.highlight: Simple Interest: $1,000,000 x 10% x 10 years = $2,000,000 | Compound Interest: $1,000,000 x (1.1)^10 = approximately $2,590,000

scenes.speech: Do you know the Rule of 72?
scenes.title: The Rule of 72
scenes.content: The number of years for your money to double = 72 ÷ annual interest rate! At 6% interest, it takes 12 years to double. At 12% interest, it takes 6 years to double. Conversely, if the inflation rate is 3%, then in 24 years, the value of your money will be halved!
scenes.highlight: The Rule of 72: 3% interest → 24 years to double | 6% interest → 12 years to double | 12% interest → 6 years to double

scenes.speech: The earlier you start, the more advantageous it is!
scenes.title: The Earlier You Start, The Better
scenes.content: Person A: starts saving $10,000 per year at age 20 (7% interest) → at age 60, approximately $26,000,000. Person B: starts saving $20,000 per year at age 30 (7% interest) → at age 60, approximately $24,000,000. With half the investment, Person A has more! Time is your greatest ally!
scenes.highlight: Starting at 20 (saving $10,000/month) vs starting at 30 (saving $20,000/month) → starting at 20 is $200,000 more!

scenes.speech: If you double a 1-yen coin every day, how much will it be after 30 days?
scenes.title: Exponential Growth
scenes.content: If you double 1 yen every day → after 30 days, it's approximately $10,000,000! Compound interest leads to 'exponential growth' - it's slow at first, but accelerates rapidly.

scenes.speech: The enemies of compound interest are fees and time!
scenes.title: Maximizing Compound Interest
scenes.content: Tips to maximize compound interest: ① Start early ② Keep fees low ③ Use tax-advantaged accounts (e.g. NISA) ④ Don't withdraw. The 4 keys are time, low costs, tax advantages, and consistency!
scenes.highlight: A 1% difference in fees can lead to hundreds of thousands of dollars in difference after 30 years → low-cost index funds are advantageous

scenes.speech: Warren Buffett's secret is compound interest!
scenes.title: Buffett and Compound Interest
scenes.content: The 'Oracle of Omaha' Warren Buffett earned over 99% of his wealth after age 60! He achieved this by compounding his wealth at approximately 20% interest per year for 65 years. With compound interest and time, anyone can build wealth!
scenes.highlight: Buffett's wealth: $114 at age 11 → $11.4 billion at age 92 (20% compound interest x 80 years)

scenes.title: Calculating Compound Interest
scenes.content: What if you invest $5,000 per month at 5% interest for 30 years? Try calculating it with a financial calculator or a 'compound interest calculator' on a bank or brokerage website!
scenes.highlight: Saving $5,000/month at 5% interest x 30 years = $1,800,000 principal → approximately $4,160,000 (about 2.3x)

scenes.speech: Be careful of compound interest on debt!
scenes.title: Beware of Compound Interest on Debt
scenes.content: Compound interest can be both a friend and a foe. Credit card debt with 15-18% interest can snowball rapidly! If you leave $100,000 debt with 18% interest for 5 years, it will become over $220,000!
scenes.highlight: Credit card debt at 18% interest: $100,000 → $220,000 in 5 years, $480,000 in 10 years!

scenes.speech: Master the magic of compound interest! Next, let's learn about cryptocurrency 🎉
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
